author: doll-agent
content:
Done. Rust's str::split('') returns the surprising ['', 'a', 'b', 'c', ''] pattern. numpy raises ValueError on empty separator. ferray was returning Rust's pattern verbatim, silently ballooning the output shape.

Added validate_separator helper returning FerrayError::InvalidValue. Called from both split() and split_ragged().

4 tests: empty separator errs in both functions; single-char and multi-char separators still work as negative controls.

ferray-strings: 147 -> 151 lib tests, all passing, clippy clean. Commit 53473b31.
